title: Rosa and the Missing Treats
synopsis: When treats go missing from the animal shelter where Rosa volunteers, she must solve the mystery by following clues and discovering that sometimes the culprit isn't who you'd expect.

pages.text: Rosa loved visiting the Sunny Paws Animal Shelter. Every Saturday, she helped Mrs. Chen feed the animals. Rosa had curly brown hair and always wore her lucky purple sneakers. 'Good morning, Rosa!' called Mrs. Chen. She was a kind woman with short black hair and a big smile. 'The animals are waiting for breakfast!'

illustration.description: A young Latina girl with curly brown hair and purple sneakers walking into a bright, colorful animal shelter. Mrs. Chen, an Asian woman with short black hair, waves from behind a reception desk covered with animal photos. Sunlight streams through windows showing pet adoption posters.
illustration.alt: Rosa entering the animal shelter and greeting Mrs. Chen

pages.text: Rosa skipped to the treat cabinet. But when she opened it, her eyes grew wide. 'Mrs. Chen!' Rosa called. 'All the dog treats are gone!' Mrs. Chen hurried over. The big jar that held crunchy bone treats was completely empty. 'That's strange,' said Mrs. Chen, scratching her head. 'I filled it yesterday!'

illustration.description: Rosa standing in front of an open cabinet with empty shelves and containers. Her expression shows surprise with wide eyes and an open mouth. Mrs. Chen looks puzzled behind her. The cabinet has colorful labels but no treats inside.
illustration.alt: Rosa discovering the empty treat cabinet

pages.text: The dogs were barking loudly in their kennels. Buster, a big golden retriever, wagged his tail. Luna, a spotted dalmatian, jumped up and down. 'Where are our treats?' their barks seemed to say. Rosa put her hands on her hips. 'Don't worry, friends. I'll solve this mystery!' she declared.

illustration.description: Rosa standing confidently with hands on hips in front of several dog kennels. Buster, a golden retriever, and Luna, a dalmatian, are visible behind colorful kennel gates, looking excited. Other diverse dogs peek out from neighboring kennels in the bright, cheerful shelter.
illustration.alt: Rosa promising to solve the mystery while dogs watch from their kennels

pages.text: Rosa became a detective. She looked around the shelter for clues. 'Aha!' she said, pointing at the floor. There were small brown crumbs leading from the cabinet to the back door. 'The treat thief went this way!' Rosa followed the trail like a bloodhound. She crawled on her hands and knees, following each tiny crumb.

illustration.description: Rosa on her hands and knees following a trail of brown crumbs across a colorful tiled floor. She has a determined expression with a magnifying glass in her hand. The trail leads from the cabinet toward a door in the background.
illustration.alt: Rosa following the trail of treat crumbs like a detective

pages.text: The crumbs led to the shelter's backyard. Rosa opened the door and stepped outside. Mr. Patel, the mailman, was walking by with his bag. 'Good morning, Rosa!' he waved. 'Have you seen anything strange today?' Rosa asked. Mr. Patel thought for a moment. 'Well, I did hear some crunching sounds by the fence earlier.'

illustration.description: Rosa in the sunny backyard talking to Mr. Patel, a friendly Indian man in a blue postal uniform carrying a mail bag. The yard has green grass, a wooden fence, and colorful flowers. Birds perch on fence posts in the bright morning light.
illustration.alt: Rosa talking to Mr. Patel the mailman in the backyard

pages.text: Rosa walked toward the fence. The crumb trail continued under a big oak tree. She looked up into the branches. Something was moving! 'What's up there?' Rosa wondered out loud. Then she heard a familiar chattering sound. 'Chitter-chatter-chitter!' Rosa gasped. She knew that sound! It was coming from way up high in the tree.

illustration.description: Rosa looking up at a large oak tree with thick branches and green leaves. Dappled sunlight filters through the canopy. Rosa shields her eyes with her hand, squinting upward with curiosity. Small shadows move among the branches above.
illustration.alt: Rosa looking up into the oak tree searching for the source of the sound

pages.text: Rosa called up to the tree. 'Hello up there! Are you the treat thief?' Suddenly, a gray squirrel poked its head out from the leaves. In its tiny paws was a dog treat! 'Chitter-chitter!' said the squirrel, as if to say 'Sorry!' Behind the first squirrel, Rosa saw three baby squirrels. They were all munching on dog treats!

illustration.description: A mama gray squirrel with expressive big eyes sitting on a tree branch, holding a bone-shaped dog treat. Three smaller baby squirrels surround her, also holding treats. Rosa waves up at them from below with a delighted expression. Sunlight creates a warm, golden glow.
illustration.alt: The squirrel family in the tree with the stolen dog treats

pages.text: Rosa started laughing. 'You took the treats for your babies!' she said to the mama squirrel. The squirrel chattered back, sounding apologetic. Rosa ran back to tell Mrs. Chen. 'The mystery is solved!' Rosa announced. 'A mama squirrel needed food for her family!' Mrs. Chen smiled. 'Well, that's not really stealing then, is it?'

illustration.description: Rosa and Mrs. Chen standing together in the shelter, both smiling warmly. Rosa gestures excitedly while explaining her discovery. Behind them, the dogs watch from their kennels with happy, curious expressions. The scene is bright and cheerful with rainbow-colored pet toys scattered around.
illustration.alt: Rosa excitedly telling Mrs. Chen about solving the mystery

pages.text: Mrs. Chen had a wonderful idea. 'Let's put a special feeding station outside for our squirrel family!' she said. Rosa clapped her hands. 'And we can get new treats for the dogs too!' From that day on, the squirrel family had their own food station. The dogs got their treats back. And Rosa? She became the shelter's official mystery solver! Everyone was happy at Sunny Paws Animal Shelter.

illustration.description: A heartwarming scene showing Rosa and Mrs. Chen setting up a small wooden feeding station under the oak tree. The squirrel family watches from the branches above while happy dogs play in the fenced yard. Colorful flowers bloom around the feeding station in the golden afternoon sunlight.
illustration.alt: Rosa and Mrs. Chen setting up the feeding station for the squirrel family

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from the animal shelter?
questions.explanation: Rosa discovered that all the dog treats were missing from the big jar in the cabinet.

questions.question: How did Rosa track down the treat thief?
questions.explanation: Rosa found brown crumbs on the floor and followed them like a detective trail.

questions.question: Who was the real treat thief?
questions.explanation: A mama squirrel had taken the treats to feed her three baby squirrels in the oak tree.

questions.question: How did Mrs. Chen solve the problem?
choices: She set up a feeding station
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen suggested putting a special feeding station outside for the squirrel family.

questions.question: What did Rosa become at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Rosa became the shelter's official mystery solver after successfully solving the case of the missing treats.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence from Rosa's mystery!

sentences.text: Rosa loved visiting the Sunny Paws Animal ______.
All the dog ______ were missing from the cabinet!
Rosa followed the ______ trail like a detective.

pages.title: Mystery Match
pages.instructions: Match the clues Rosa found with what they told her!

sentences.text: The ______ squirrel took treats for her babies.
Mrs. Chen set up a ______ station for the squirrels.
